prompt.content: You are a workflow automation agent. Execute the requested tasks using the available tools. Do not ask clarifying questions - use the information provided and make reasonable assumptions when needed.
Route the pending employee grievances to the appropriate handler. Update each case status and send notification emails. Follow our grievance handling procedures. Use Google Sheets and Gmail.

messages.subject: Employee Grievance Handling Procedures - 2026
messages.body_plain:
EMPLOYEE GRIEVANCE HANDLING PROCEDURES

Section 1: Scope
These procedures apply to all formal grievances submitted through the HR grievance system.

Section 2: Categories
Grievances are categorized as: Workplace Conduct, Compensation, Discrimination, Safety, and Policy Dispute.

Section 3: Severity Levels
Level 1 (Low): Minor workplace disagreements. Level 2 (Medium): Recurring issues or policy violations. Level 3 (High): Serious misconduct, discrimination, or safety hazards.

Section 4: Standard Routing
- Level 1: Route to the employee's direct manager. Email manager with case details.
- Level 2: Route to HRBP (hr-bp@company.example.com). Email HRBP with case details.
- Level 3: Route to Legal (legal@company.example.com) AND HR Director (hr-director@company.example.com). Email both with case details.

Section 5: Confidentiality
All grievance information is strictly confidential. Do not post grievance details to any Slack channel.

Section 6: Special Provisions
Grievances involving a manager-subordinate relationship where the manager IS the subject of the complaint must skip the manager route entirely. These cases go directly to HRBP regardless of severity level.

Section 7: Timeline
All grievances must be acknowledged within 2 business days.

Section 8: Documentation
Update the Status column to 'Routed to [handler]' after routing.

messages.subject: Grievance Handling Update - Discrimination Cases
messages.body_plain:
Team,

Effective immediately: ALL discrimination-category grievances, regardless of severity level, must be routed to Legal AND HR Director. This overrides the standard severity-based routing for discrimination cases.

- HR Director

cells.Description: Noise complaint about adjacent team area
Manager Robert Kim making inappropriate comments
Disagrees with new remote work policy application
Passed over for promotion due to gender
